blocks:
Ayahs

وَٱلنَّـٰزِعَـٰتِ غَرْقًا (۱)
By those [angels] who wrest [the soul] violently, (1)
وَٱلنَّـٰشِطَـٰتِ نَشْطًا (۲)
by those who draw [it] out gently, (2)
وَٱلسَّـٰبِحَـٰتِ سَبْحًا (۳)
by those who swim smoothly, (3)
فَٱلسَّـٰبِقَـٰتِ سَبْقًا (٤)
by those who, racing, take the lead, (4)
فَٱلْمُدَبِّرَٰتِ أَمْرًا (٥)
by those who direct the affairs [of creatures]: (5)

Key Points

"Naza'" (نزع) means separating by uprooting and pulling. "Gharq" (غرق) and "Ighraq" (اغراق) mean intensity. "Nashat" (نشط) means untying a knot, and nishat (vitality/joy) means that spiritual knots are untied. In contrast to some humans whose prayers are performed with lethargy and whose charity is given reluctantly, the Divine angels are vigorous in carrying out their missions. "Nashit" (ناشط) refers to someone who performs a task willingly. This word is also used for someone who pulls a bucket of water out of a well, implying that the angels gently draw out the souls of the believers. (9) "Al-Nāzi'āt" (النازعات) and "Al-Nāshiṭāt" (الناشطات) are, in fact, two descriptions for a group (ṭā'ifah) of angels, and since the word ṭā'ifah is feminine, these descriptions are also presented in the feminine form. "Al-Sābiḥāt" (السابحات) derives from sabḥ, meaning rapid movement in water or air. This indicates that the angels are swift and agile in carrying out Divine missions. According to Imam Ali عليه السلام, the angels take the souls of the believers gently and kindly: «یقبضون ارواح المومنین یسلّونها سلاً رفیقا» (10) Allah's oath by the angels is both a sign of the importance and status of the angels in the cosmic order and the importance of the matter for which the oath is sworn. It is possible that the five attributes mentioned in these verses refer to five groups of angels, or they might refer to a single group, meaning that as soon as the command is issued, they detach [from their place], pursue it vigorously, move swiftly, and race one another to execute the command judiciously. (11)

9) Tafsir Rahnama. 10) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 11) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The more attached a person is to material things, the harder it will be for his soul to be detached from them. «والنازعات غرقا»

2- The moment of death is the beginning of reward and punishment. «غرقا، نشطا»

3- Every group of angels is assigned a specific task. «النازعات، الناشطات، السابحات»

4- Having vigor in carrying out a mission is a virtue. «والناشطات نشطا»

5 - The entire universe is the field of work for the angels. «والسابحات سبحاً»

6- Angels race one another in carrying out the Divine mission. «فالسابقات»

7- Angels perform their duties in the best possible manner. «غرقا، نشطاً، سبحاً، سبقاً»

8 - Although the management of the universe belongs to Allah: «یدبّر الامر» (12), Allah has also permitted the angels to manage affairs. «فالمدبّرات امرا»

9- Angels possess the necessary skill in administration and management. «فالمدبّرات امرا»

10- The management and administration of affairs require vigor, racing, and agility. «الناشطات، السابحات، السابقات، المدبّرات»

11- The cosmic order is not accidental; it is based on management (Divine planning). «فالمدبّرات...»

12- Management (or judicious planning) is a virtue by which an oath can be sworn. «فالمدبّرات امراً» (12) Yunus, 3.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah An-Nāzi‘āt

This Surah has forty-six verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of the Surah is taken from the first verse and means "The Angels Who Draw Out Souls." Like other Meccan Surahs and Surah An-Naba' which preceded it, the central theme of this Surah is the Day of Resurrection and the Return (to Allah). Since the taking of the souls of believers and disbelievers, and the management of the affairs of all servants, are entrusted to the angels, this Surah begins with an oath sworn by various types of angels. The narration of a part of the story of Prophet Moses and Pharaoh, and his rebellion and defiance against the truth—which resulted in severe Divine punishment for him in this world—serves as an introduction to a general principle: that rebellion and worldliness lead man to Hell, while struggling against carnal desires and fearing the Lord guide man to the paradise of bliss.

Ayahs

يَوْمَ تَرْجُفُ ٱلرَّاجِفَةُ (٦)
the day when the Quaker quakes (6)
تَتْبَعُهَا ٱلرَّادِفَةُ (۷)
and is followed by the Successor, (7)
قُلُوبٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ وَاجِفَةٌ (۸)
hearts will be trembling on that day, (8)
أَبْصَـٰرُهَا خَـٰشِعَةٌ (۹)
bearing a humbled look. (9)
يَقُولُونَ أَءِنَّا لَمَرْدُودُونَ فِى ٱلْحَافِرَةِ (۱٠)
They will say, ‘Are we being returned to our earlier state? (10)
أَءِذَا كُنَّا عِظَـٰمًا نَّخِرَةً (۱۱)
What, even after we have been decayed bones?!’ (11)
قَالُوا۟ تِلْكَ إِذًا كَرَّةٌ خَاسِرَةٌ (۱۲)
They will say, ‘This is, then, a ruinous return!’ (12)
فَإِنَّمَا هِىَ زَجْرَةٌ وَٰحِدَةٌ (۱۳)
Yet it will be only a single shout, (13)
فَإِذَا هُم بِٱلسَّاهِرَةِ (۱٤)
and behold, they will be awake. (14)

Key Points

Rājifah (الراجفة) means intense anxiety and tremor. Arājīf (plural) refers to seditious words that cause anxiety. Rādifah (الرادفة) is derived from radīf, meaning to follow, and refers to aftershocks. Perhaps the first earthquake is due to the first blast of the Trumpet (Sūr), causing everyone to die, and Rādifah refers to the second blast, causing everyone to be resurrected, as explicitly stated regarding both matters in Ayah 68 of Sūrat Az-Zumar. Wājifah (واجفة) refers to intense and rapid movement or anxiety, and Khāshi‘ah (خاشعة) refers to someone who has lowered their head and fixed their gaze upon the ground. Hāfirah (حافرة) means the beginning or origin of anything. Therefore, the disbelievers were astonished at returning to the first life. However, Hāfirah might mean mahfūrah (that which is dug), meaning the grave that they dig in the earth, in which case the meaning would be: Will we be returned and resurrected from our graves after dying? (13) Nakhirah (نخرة) is a decayed bone that crumbles with the slightest pressure. Hearts that tremble in fear of Allah in this world, «وجلت قلوبهم» (14), will be safe in the Hereafter. «لاخوف علیهم و لا هم یحزنون» (15). And hearts that have heedless tranquility in this world will be anxious there: «قلوب یومئذ واجفة». Zajr (زجر) is driving away with a shout or sound, and Zajrah (زجرة) is perhaps the second blast (Nafkhah) of the Trumpet, which precedes the resurrection of all the dead. And Sāhirah (ساهرة) is the level ground of the Day of Judgment, because in that land, eyes do not sleep but remain in a state of wakefulness (sahar).

13) Tafsīr al-Mīzān. 14) Al-Anfāl, 2. 15) Yūnus, 62.

Messages

1- The transformation of the natural order is among the preliminaries of the Resurrection. «ترجف الراجفة...»

2- The earthquakes of the Day of Judgment are not in a single stage; they are successive. «الراجفة... الرادفة»

3- Not everyone will be worried on the Day of Judgment; only some hearts will be anxious. «قلوبٌ»

4- A deep connection exists between the soul and the body. «قلوب... واجفة، ابصارها خاشعة»

5- A group of people deny the occurrence of the Day of Judgment by posing repetitive questions. «یقولون ءانّا لمردودون؟»

Ayahs

هَلْ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ مُوسَىٰٓ (۱٥)
Did you receive the story of Moses, (15)
إِذْ نَادَىٰهُ رَبُّهُۥ بِٱلْوَادِ ٱلْمُقَدَّسِ طُوًى (۱٦)
when his Lord called out to him in the holy valley of Tuwa? (16)
ٱذْهَبْ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّهُۥ طَغَىٰ (۱۷)
[And said,] ‘Go to Pharaoh, for indeed he has rebelled, (17)
فَقُلْ هَل لَّكَ إِلَىٰٓ أَن تَزَكَّىٰ (۱۸)
and say, ‘‘Would you purify yourself? (18)
وَأَهْدِيَكَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ فَتَخْشَىٰ (۱۹)
I will guide you to your Lord, that you may fear [Him]?’’ ’ (19)

Key Points

Wād (واد) is the valley (wādī) which refers to the gorge between two mountains or two hills, and Ṭuwā (طُوى) is a valley at the foot of Mount Ṭūr. Khashyah (خشیت) refers to a fear that arises from faith in the Greatness of Allah. Tazakkā (تَزكّى), derived from Zakāt (زكات), has two meanings: growth and development, and purity and cleanliness (ṭahārah). The place of conversation with Allah must be pure and sacred. Just as He says in this Ayah: «ناداه ربّه بالواد المقدّس» (His Lord called him in the sacred valley), and in other Ayahs we read: «طهّر بیتى للطائفین و القائمین و الرّكع السجود»(16), (Allah said to Abraham:) "Purify My House for those who circumambulate, and those who stand (in prayer), and those who bow and prostrate (the worshippers)." «ما كان للمشركین ان یعمروا مساجد اللّه»(17), The polytheists are not worthy and have no right to undertake the maintenance and reconstruction of the Mosques of Allah. «ان اولیائه الاّ المتقون»(18), Only the pious are worthy of the guardianship and custodianship (of the Sacred Mosque). «خذوا زینتكم عند كلّ مسجد»(19), Take your adornment at every mosque and during prayer, take your adornments with you.

16) Hajj, 26. 17) Tawbah, 17. 18) Anfal, 34. 19) A'raf, 31.

Messages

1- Beginning a speech with a question increases the desire to listen in people. «هل اتاك»

2- Narrating the history of previous prophets to subsequent prophets is a factor in strengthening morale. «هل اتاك حدیث موسى»

3- Divine leaders would strengthen their connection with Allah before confronting tyrants. «اذ ناداه ربّه... اذهب الى فرعون انه طغى»

4- Some lands are respected and sacred. «الواد المقدّس طوى»

5- The Prophets are the leaders in the struggle against tyranny. «اذهب الى فرعون انه طغى»

6- In forbidding evil (Nahy ‘anil-Munkar), go after the roots. «اذهب الى فرعون»

7- Do not despair of anyone's guidance, even Pharaoh, and at least convey the word of truth to him so that the argument is completed (itmaam al-hujjah). «اذهب الى فرعون... فقل هل لك الى ان تزكّى»

8- In forbidding evil, speak gently, even with tyrants. «انه طغى فقل هل لك...»

9- Awaken innate natures (fiṭrah) through questioning. «هل لك الى ان تزكّى»

10- In the method of propagation (tablīgh), do not forget the emotional tone and softness in speech. «هل لك»

11- The purpose of the Prophets' invitation is the purification of human beings. «هل لك الى ان تزكّى»

12- In inviting and propagating to people, use words that all human beings accept and approve of. (The invitation to purity and distance from impurity) «الى ان تزكّى»

13- If there is no inclination towards purification, the efforts of the Prophets are fruitless. «تزكّى و اهدیك الى ربّك»

14- In the school of the Prophets, before fighting tyrants, they are first admonished. «انّه طغى... تزكّى و اهدیك الى ربّك»

15- The sign of accepting guidance is fear and reverence (khashyah). «اهدیك... فتخشى»

16- The Prophets invited people towards Allah, not towards themselves. «الى ربّك»

17- Tyranny (ṭughyān) is accompanied by recklessness and impurity. (Allah says: Go to the tyrannical Pharaoh and speak to him about purification and reverence, meaning he is impure and audacious.) «انّه طغى ... ان تزكّى... فتخشى»

18- The reform of individuals depends on their own will and choice. «هل لك الى ان تزكىّ»

Ayahs

فَأَرَىٰهُ ٱلْـَٔايَةَ ٱلْكُبْرَىٰ (۲٠)
Then he showed him the greatest sign. (20)
فَكَذَّبَ وَعَصَىٰ (۲۱)
But he denied, and disobeyed. (21)
ثُمَّ أَدْبَرَ يَسْعَىٰ (۲۲)
Then he turned back, walking swiftly, (22)
فَحَشَرَ فَنَادَىٰ (۲۳)
and gathered [the people] and proclaimed, (23)
فَقَالَ أَنَا۠ رَبُّكُمُ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ (۲٤)
saying, ‘I am your exalted lord!’ (24)
فَأَخَذَهُ ٱللَّهُ نَكَالَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَٱلْأُولَىٰٓ (۲٥)
So Allah seized him with the punishment of this life and the Hereafter. (25)
إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَعِبْرَةً لِّمَن يَخْشَىٰٓ (۲٦)
There is indeed a moral in that for someone who fears! (26)

Key Points

| In response to Pharaoh who said: «اَنا ربّكم الاعلى» (I am your Lord, the Most High), Prophet Moses says: «اهدیك الى ربّك» (I guide you to your Lord). Nakal (نكال) is a penalty and punishment such that whoever sees or hears of it refrains from committing anything similar. The punishment of the Hereafter is called Nakal because whoever hears of it refrains from any action that would subject him to it.(20)

20) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- For people like Pharaoh, the greatest miracles must be presented. «الآیة الكبرى»

2- Tyrants (Taghuts) seek help from the misguided to preserve their domain. «فحشر فنادى»

3- Claiming prophethood and expecting others to submit requires presenting a miracle. «اهدیك... فاراه الآیة الكبرى»

4- For some arrogant people, even the greatest miracles are ineffective. «الآیة الكبرى فكذّب»

5- The miracles of the Prophets are not uniform. «الآیة الكبرى»

6- Denying the Prophets is motivated by the desire to disobey their teachings and be free from religious constraints. «فكذّب و عصى»

7- Those who are incapable of direct argumentation and dialogue strive behind the scenes. «فكذّب... ثمّ ادبر یسعى»

8- Creating atmosphere, slogans, and commotion are among the tools of the Pharaohs. «فحشر فنادى»

9- People possess an innate God-seeking nature (Fitrah). Tyrants redirect this path toward themselves. «انا ربّكم»

10- The arrogant will one day be brought to their knees. «انا ربّكم... فاَخَذَه اللّه»

11- History is a means of taking lessons (Ibrah), not entertainment or filling leisure time. «لعبرةً»

12- Recount the part of history that is constructive and instructive. «لعبرةً»

13- Lessons are numerous; taking the lesson is what is important, and this requires spiritual readiness. «لعبرةً لمن یخشى»

Ayahs

ءَأَنتُمْ أَشَدُّ خَلْقًا أَمِ ٱلسَّمَآءُ ۚ بَنَىٰهَا (۲۷)
Is your creation more prodigious or that of the heaven He has built? (27)
رَفَعَ سَمْكَهَا فَسَوَّىٰهَا (۲۸)
He raised its vault and fashioned it, (28)
وَأَغْطَشَ لَيْلَهَا وَأَخْرَجَ ضُحَىٰهَا (۲۹)
and darkened its night, and brought forth its forenoon. (29)
وَٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ دَحَىٰهَآ (۳٠)
Thereafter He spread out the earth, (30)
أَخْرَجَ مِنْهَا مَآءَهَا وَمَرْعَىٰهَا (۳۱)
brought forth from it its water and pastures, (31)
وَٱلْجِبَالَ أَرْسَىٰهَا (۳۲)
setting firm the mountains, (32)
مَتَـٰعًا لَّكُمْ وَلِأَنْعَـٰمِكُمْ (۳۳)
as a [place of] sustenance for you and your livestock. (33)

Messages

1- In propagation, the method of questioning is very useful. «ءانتم اشد خلقاً ام السماء»

2- The best way to know Allah is attention to His blessings. One must use tangible things to understand abstract concepts. «رفع سمكها فسوّاها»

3- The world is for us, not we for the world. «متاعاً لكم»

4- In material enjoyments, humans and livestock are side by side. (What distinguishes humans are spiritual perfections.) «متاعاً لكم و لانعامكم»

5- Blessings are for human utilization; undue prohibitions and asceticism are meaningless. «متاعا لكم و لانعامكم»

6- The spreading of the earth and its rotation in space occurred after the creation of the heavens. «و الارض بعد ذلك دَحاها»

Ayahs

فَإِذَا جَآءَتِ ٱلطَّآمَّةُ ٱلْكُبْرَىٰ (۳٤)
When the Greatest Catastrophe befalls (34)
يَوْمَ يَتَذَكَّرُ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ مَا سَعَىٰ (۳٥)
—the day when man will remember his endeavours (35)
وَبُرِّزَتِ ٱلْجَحِيمُ لِمَن يَرَىٰ (۳٦)
and hell is brought into view for those who can see— (36)
فَأَمَّا مَن طَغَىٰ (۳۷)
as for him who has been rebellious (37)
وَءَاثَرَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا (۳۸)
and preferred the life of this world, (38)
فَإِنَّ ٱلْجَحِيمَ هِىَ ٱلْمَأْوَىٰ (۳۹)
his refuge will indeed be hell. (39)
وَأَمَّا مَنْ خَافَ مَقَامَ رَبِّهِۦ وَنَهَى ٱلنَّفْسَ عَنِ ٱلْهَوَىٰ (٤٠)
But as for him who is awed to stand before his Lord and restrains his soul from [following] desires, (40)
فَإِنَّ ٱلْجَنَّةَ هِىَ ٱلْمَأْوَىٰ (٤۱)
his refuge will indeed be paradise. (41)

Messages

1- The Resurrection (Qiyamah) is the greatest and most comprehensive event that overshadows all other events. «الطامّة الكبرى»

2- On the Day of Resurrection, man will constantly remember the deeds he performed in the world. «یتذكّر الانسان ما سعى»

3- The root of transgression is worldliness. «طغى و آثر الحیاة الدنیا»

4- The world itself is not bad; preferring it over the Hereafter is what is bad. «آثر الحیاة الدنیا»

5- Allah Himself is not fearful; it is His station (His authority) that causes man to fear. It is like a person fearing a judge because he knows that if he commits a crime, he will have to deal with Him. «خاف مقام ربّه»

6- The root of struggling against the self is the fear of Allah. «خاف مقام ربّه و نهى النفس عن الهوى»

7- The human self (nafs) is rebellious (a source of temptation) and must be restrained. «و نهى النفس عن الهوى»

8- The Divine law is the same for all individuals. «من طغى... من خاف»

Ayahs

يَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلسَّاعَةِ أَيَّانَ مُرْسَىٰهَا (٤۲)
They ask you concerning the Hour, “When will it set in, (42)
فِيمَ أَنتَ مِن ذِكْرَىٰهَآ (٤۳)
considering your frequent mention of it?” (43)
إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ مُنتَهَىٰهَآ (٤٤)
Its outcome is with your Lord. (44)
إِنَّمَآ أَنتَ مُنذِرُ مَن يَخْشَىٰهَا (٤٥)
You are only a warner for those who are afraid it. (45)
كَأَنَّهُمْ يَوْمَ يَرَوْنَهَا لَمْ يَلْبَثُوٓا۟ إِلَّا عَشِيَّةً أَوْ ضُحَىٰهَا (٤٦)
The day they see it, it shall be as if they had not stayed [in the world] except for an evening or forenoon. (46)

Key Points

The question regarding the time of the occurrence of the Hour (Qiyamah) has been raised repeatedly in the Quran, but no answer has been given to it. This is because one of Allah's graces is the concealment of the time of death and the Hour. Knowing its time would disrupt inner peace and external relationships, and would lead to forced (or compulsory) growth. What is important is faith in the occurrence of the Hour, not knowing the time of its occurrence. For the heedless, warning (Inthar) is more necessary than glad tidings (Bisharah). Therefore, we do not find "انما انت مبشّر" (You are only a bearer of glad tidings) in the Quran, but "انّما انت منذر" (You are only a warner) is mentioned.

Messages

1- Some questions about the Hour are inappropriate and do not require an answer (such as the question about the time of its occurrence). «یسئلونك عن الساعة...»

2- Discussion about a matter that does not concern humanity is meaningless. «فیم انت من ذكراها»

3- Human knowledge is limited and cannot reach the ultimate limit of knowledge, which belongs exclusively to Allah. «الى ربّك منتهاها»

4- The duty of the Prophet is to warn people, not to compel or force them to accept. «انّما انت منذر»

5- Among the conditions for accepting the call of the Prophets is the spiritual readiness of the people and fearing sin (having piety). «انت منذر من یخشاها»

6- The Hereafter is so immense that the entire lifespan of this world and the Barzakh (intermediate realm), compared to it, is no more than the duration of an evening or a morning. «لم یلبثوا الاّ عشیةً او ضحاها»
